name: Pradhan Mantri Mudra Yojana (PMMY)
description: Funding for non-corporate, non-farm small/micro enterprises with loans up to ₹20 Lakhs.

details.purpose: Provides micro-credit for income-generating activities in manufacturing, trading, and service sectors.

eligibility: Must be an Indian citizen between 18 and 65 years old.
Business must be in manufacturing, trading, or services (including allied agriculture).
Must not be a defaulter to any bank.
benefits: Collateral-free loans
No processing fee for Shishu/Kishore loans
Credit guaranteed by CGFMU
target: Small entrepreneurs, startup founders, shopkeepers

name: CGTMSE
description: Credit Guarantee Fund Trust for Micro and Small Enterprises providing collateral-free credit.

details.purpose: Facilitates collateral-free credit to Micro and Small Enterprises (MSEs) by providing guarantee cover to lenders.

eligibility: Must be a Micro or Small Enterprise (new or existing).
Viable business track record and not a bank defaulter.
Valid Udyam Registration and IT-PAN is mandatory.
benefits: Access to massive credit without collateral
Reduced risk for lenders encourages approval
Special enhanced limits for women entrepreneurs
target: Manufacturing and services MSEs requiring high capital

name: Stand Up India
description: Facilitating bank loans between 10 lakh and 1 Crore to at least one SC/ST borrower and one woman borrower per bank branch.

details.purpose: Promotes entrepreneurship among women and SC/ST communities by facilitating greenfield enterprise setup.
details.interestRate: Lowest applicable rate (Max: MCLR + 3% + tenor premium)
details.repaymentTenure: Up to 7 years with max 18 months moratorium

categories.name: Working Capital
categories.limit: Via Rupay debit card or Cash Credit

eligibility: Exclusively for Women and SC/ST entrepreneurs above 18 years.
Must be a first-time 'Greenfield' project in manufacturing, services, or trading.
For companies, 51% controlling stake must be held by Woman or SC/ST.
benefits: Secures funding for traditionally underserved demographics
Comprehensive handholding support and training
Covers up to 85% of total project cost
target: Women and SC/ST entrepreneurs launching first venture

name: PMEGP
description: Prime Minister's Employment Generation Programme providing credit-linked subsidies to generate self-employment.

details.purpose: A credit-linked subsidy scheme meant to generate employment opportunities by helping set up new micro-enterprises.
details.interestRate: Standard bank term loan rates after subsidy
details.repaymentTenure: Varies (Margin money kept in lock-in for 3 years)

eligibility: Individuals above 18 years aiming to start a NEW project.
Must be 8th pass for Manufacturing > ₹10L or Service > ₹5L.
Family restriction: Only one person per family can avail the benefit.
benefits: Up to 35% subsidy on project cost for special categories in rural areas.
Only 5-10% beneficiary contribution needed.
Second loan provision up to ₹1 Crore for successful units.
target: Unemployed youth, rural and urban artisans

name: ZED Certification Scheme
description: Zero Defect Zero Effect (ZED) model to motivate MSMEs to manufacture quality products with zero environmental effect.

details.purpose: Aims to enhance competitiveness by motivating MSMEs to adopt best practices in quality (Zero Defect) and environmental responsibility (Zero Effect).

eligibility: All MSMEs registered with the UDYAM registration portal.
Manufacturing activity must be operational.
Must take the 'ZED Pledge' before applying.
benefits: Substantial subsidies (up to 95%) on certification costs (Bronze/Silver/Gold).
Financial assistance up to ₹5 Lakhs for handholding and technology upgrades.
Better credit access and preference in public procurement.
target: Manufacturing MSMEs

name: Startup India Seed Fund (SISFS)
description: Financial assistance to startups for proof of concept, prototype development, product trials, and market entry.

details.purpose: Provides early-stage financial assistance to startups through incubators to help them reach a level where they can raise investments from angel investors or VCs.
details.interestRate: Max at prevailing repo rate (for debt instruments)

categories.name: Stage 1 Grant
categories.limit: Up to ₹20 Lakhs for Proof of Concept & Prototypes

categories.name: Stage 2 Investment
categories.limit: Up to ₹50 Lakhs for Market Entry & Commercialization (Debt/Convertible)

eligibility: Must be a DPIIT-recognized startup incorporated less than 2 years ago.
Must have a business idea with viable commercialization and scaling scope.
Must not have received > ₹10 Lakhs under any other Govt scheme.
benefits: Crucial early-stage milestone-based non-dilutive grant funding.
Unsecured debt funding for commercialization.
Access to India's top incubator networks.
target: Early-stage startup founders

name: TReDS (Trade Receivables Discounting System)
description: Electronic platform for MSMEs to manage their working capital by discounting trade receivables.

details.purpose: Facilitates the financing and discounting of trade receivables for MSMEs from corporate buyers through multiple financiers.
details.maxAmount: Depends entirely on accepted invoice value
details.repaymentTenure: Buyer pays financier on invoice due date

categories.name: Factoring Unit
categories.limit: Invoice uploaded by MSME or Buyer

categories.name: Financier Bidding
categories.limit: Banks/NBFCs bid to discount the invoice

eligibility: Must be an MSME with valid Udyam Registration, GST, and PAN.
Must have receivables from large Corporates, Government Depts, or PSUs.
Must be operational for at least one year.
benefits: Immediate Cash Flow: Converts invoices to cash in 24-48 hours.
Without Recourse: MSME is not liable if the corporate buyer defaults.
Debt-Free: Does not add to the MSME's loan liabilities on the balance sheet.
target: Any MSME supplier to large corporates or PSUs

name: SIDBI Make in India Loan (SMILE)
description: Soft Loan Fund for Enterprises to provide quasi-equity and soft loans to MSMEs.

details.purpose: Aims to foster innovation, attract investment, and establish top-tier manufacturing infrastructure by assisting MSMEs with debt-equity ratio requirements.
details.collateralRequired: First charge over project assets + Personal guarantees

categories.name: Term Loan
categories.limit: Up to 75% of project cost (Min ₹50 Lakhs for new)

eligibility: All MSMEs, focusing on new manufacturing and service enterprises.
Existing businesses undertaking expansion or technology modernization.
Targeting 25 identified 'Make in India' sectors.
benefits: Very competitive, fixed interest rates for the crucial first 3 years.
Long repayment tenure of 10 years with a comfortable 3-year moratorium.
Enhanced soft loan limits (15%) for SC/ST/Women entrepreneurs.
target: Expansion and new projects under Make in India

name: Digital MSME Scheme
description: Promoting Information and Communication Technology (ICT) in MSME sector for improved efficiency.

details.purpose: Aims to transition traditional MSMEs into 'Digital MSMEs' by integrating ICT into production and business processes (cloud computing, ERP, digital marketing).

eligibility: Must be registered with a valid Udyam Registration.
Must possess valid GSTIN and PAN.
Must not have availed similar Govt IT adoption benefits previously.
benefits: Financial assistance up to ₹1 Lakh over two years for cloud/IT adoption.
Increases productivity and lowers operational costs.
Enhances competitiveness in national and international markets.
target: Businesses looking to digitize their operations

name: Credit Linked Capital Subsidy Scheme (CLCSS)
description: Facilitating technology upgradation by providing 15% upfront capital subsidy.

details.purpose: Helps Micro and Small Enterprises upgrade their technology by providing an upfront capital subsidy on institutional finance for new plant and machinery.
details.repaymentTenure: Linked to the underlying bank term loan
details.collateralRequired: Governed by the underlying bank term loan

eligibility: Must be a registered Micro or Small unit (Medium enterprises not eligible).
Must avail a term loan from an eligible bank for new/upgraded machinery.
Technology adoption must be a demonstrable upgrade for productivity/quality.
benefits: Massive upfront capital subsidy up to ₹15 Lakhs (₹25 Lakhs for SC/ST).
Encourages modernization without crushing initial capital burdens.
Covers over 50 specific industrial sub-sectors.
target: Manufacturing units requiring machinery upgrades

name: MSME Champions Scheme
description: An umbrella scheme integrating MSME-Innovative, MSME-Sustainable (ZED), and MSME-Competitive (Lean).

details.purpose: A comprehensive initiative to modernize processes, reduce wastage, and facilitate the growth of MSMEs by integrating multiple support programs.

eligibility: Must be registered on the Udyam portal.
Both manufacturing and service sector MSMEs can apply.
Individual innovators attached to Host Institutes are also eligible.
benefits: Direct financial support for technology upgrades, certifications, and consultancy.
Funding and guidance for new product development and IPR.
Single-window grievance redressal via the Champions portal.
target: Aggressive, growth-oriented MSMEs seeking global standards

name: National SC/ST Hub (NSSH)
description: Supporting SC/ST entrepreneurs to fulfill the 4% procurement requirement by Central Government.

details.purpose: Provides end-to-end support to SC/ST entrepreneurs, helping them establish businesses and participate effectively in public procurement (GeM).

categories.name: Financial Subsidy
categories.limit: Testing fee, Bank loan processing reimbursements

eligibility: Must unequivocally belong to a Scheduled Caste (SC) or Scheduled Tribe (ST).
At least 51% ownership and control must lie with SC/ST individuals.
Must be a registered Micro or Small Enterprise on Udyam.
benefits: 100% subsidy for NSIC Single Point Registration.
80% reimbursement for bank loan processing fees and testing fees.
25% Capital Subsidy for purchasing plant and machinery.
target: SC/ST business owners seeking to expand and secure Govt contracts

name: Lean Manufacturing Competitiveness
description: Financial assistance for implementing Lean manufacturing techniques to reduce waste.
benefits: 80% financial support for consultant fees
Waste reduction
Increased productivity
eligibility: MSMEs in clusters or groups
target: Cluster-based manufacturing units

name: Cluster Development Programme
description: Enhancing productivity and competitiveness by creating Common Facility Centres (CFCs).
benefits: Grant up to 70-90% for CFCs
Marketing hubs support
Common raw material bank
eligibility: State governments or SPVs of MSMEs
target: Groups of MSMEs in same sector

name: Mahila Coir Yojana
description: Exclusive training and subsidy scheme for women in the coir industry.
benefits: Stipend during training
75% subsidy on machinery
Empowerment focused
eligibility: Women artisans in coir sector
target: Rural women entrepreneurs

name: ASPIRE Scheme
description: Scheme for Promotion of Innovation, Rural Industries and Entrepreneurship.
benefits: Support for Livelihood Business Incubators (LBIs)
Funding for Technology Business Incubators
eligibility: Trusts, non-profits, governmental agencies
target: Rural innovators and educators

name: SFURTI
description: Scheme of Fund for Regeneration of Traditional Industries.
benefits: Modernization of clusters
Skill development for artisans
Market linkages
eligibility: Artisans clusters
NGOs with experience in traditional sectors
target: Traditional sectors like bamboo, honey, khadi

name: Interest Subvention Scheme
description: 2% interest subvention for incremental credit to MSMEs.
benefits: Direct reduction in interest cost
Support for valid GST registered MSMEs
eligibility: Udyam and GST registered units
target: Growth-stage MSMEs

name: MSME Samadhaan
description: Official portal for MSMEs to file cases for delayed payments from buyers.
benefits: Legal backing for payments
Interest on delayed payments
Pressure on large buyers
eligibility: Udyam registered MSEs
target: MSEs facing liquidity crunch due to delays

name: International Cooperation (IC) Scheme
description: Financial assistance to MSMEs for participating in international exhibitions, trade fairs, and buyer-seller meets.

details.purpose: Aims to enhance the global competitiveness of Indian MSMEs by facilitating their participation in international trade activities and export promotion.
details.maxAmount: Variable (e.g., up to ₹3.00 Lakh for space rent, ₹1.5L for airfare)

categories.name: Delegation Visits
categories.limit: Support for exploring new technologies abroad

eligibility: Assistance is sought by eligible MSME industry associations/Govt organizations.
MSME units must score at least 60% on the scheme's eligibility scorecard.
Must possess Udyam Registration.
benefits: Reimbursement of 100% economy airfare (up to ₹1.5 Lakh).
Space rent subsidy up to ₹3.00 Lakh per MSME unit for foreign exhibitions.
Exposure to international markets and joint venture opportunities.
target: Export-oriented MSMEs

name: Entrepreneurship and Skill Development Programme (ESDP)
description: Comprehensive training programs to foster an entrepreneurial ecosystem and enhance workforce skills.

details.purpose: Aims to motivate youth representing different sections of society including SC/ST/Women/BPL to consider self-employment or entrepreneurship as one of the career options.

eligibility: Minimum age is 18 years (relaxable in special cases).
Preference given to SC, ST, women, ex-servicemen, differently-abled, and BPL.
Open to existing entrepreneurs wanting to upgrade management skills.
benefits: No fee is charged from SC, ST, women, physically handicapped, and BPL candidates.
Stipends sometimes provided for candidates from weaker sections.
Technical, operational, and management skills training.
target: Aspiring startup founders and existing MSME owners

name: Technology Upgradation Scheme (TEQUP)
description: Financial support for implementing Energy Efficient Technologies (EETs) in MSMEs.

details.purpose: Focuses on promoting Energy Efficient Technologies (EET) among MSMEs to reduce carbon footprint and operational costs.

eligibility: MSME unit with Entrepreneurial Memorandum or Udyam.
Must have an energy audit and Detailed Project Report (DPR) by a qualified Auditor.
Usually applied through SIDBI or eligible banks.
benefits: Financial support of 25% of the project cost for EET.
Massive reductions in long-term energy and operational costs.
Compliance with global environmental sustainability metrics.
target: Manufacturing MSMEs with high energy footprints

name: Micro and Small Enterprises Cluster Development Programme (MSE-CDP)
description: Funding for Common Facility Centres (CFCs) and Infrastructure Development for MSME clusters.

details.purpose: Enhances the competitiveness of MSEs by supporting the creation of common infrastructure, shared facilities (testing, training, design), and industrial areas.
details.maxAmount: Up to Rs. 30.00 Crore (for CFC project cost limit)

categories.name: Common Facility Centres
categories.limit: GoI grant up to 70%-80% of project cost

categories.name: Infrastructure Development
categories.limit: GoI grant up to 60%-70% of project cost

eligibility: At least 20 MSEs in a cluster must form a Special Purpose Vehicle (SPV).
A single unit cannot hold more than 10% equity in the SPV.
Individual MSMEs cannot apply directly.
benefits: Grants of 70% to 80% for creating shared assets like R&D, testing, and effluent treatment plants.
Grants for developing roads, power, and water infrastructure for the cluster.
Massive reduction in independent capital expenditure for small units.
target: Geographic Clusters of Micro and Small Enterprises

name: Procurement and Marketing Support (PMS) Scheme
description: Financial assistance for trade fairs, packaging, barcoding, and e-commerce adoption.

details.purpose: Enhances the marketability of products/services by providing funding for trade fairs, modern packaging, barcoding, and digital marketing/e-commerce.
details.maxAmount: Variable (e.g., up to ₹1.5L per Trade Fair)

eligibility: Must be a registered Micro or Small Enterprise (MSE) with Udyam Registration.
Must be actively engaged in manufacturing or services.
Preference for Women/SC/ST/NER/Aspirational Districts.
benefits: Reimbursement of stall rent & travel for 2 domestic/international trade fairs a year.
Financial support for modern packaging, GI stores, and barcoding registration.
Connects MSMEs directly to CPSEs via Vendor Development Programs.
target: MSEs struggling with market outreach and branding
